description:
Advances: mutagen.mutation_pipeline, mutagen.coverage

Closes **F13** (HIGH, Testing reviewer). The r11 disk-write tests in mutation_runner_test.exs:729-753 + coverage_runner_test.exs:283-301 only hash `lib/**/*.ex` to assert no side-effect writes. They miss `_build`, `cover/`, `/tmp`, host project config files. The bytecode-identical-restore invariant is broader than file content.

## Deliverable
- Broaden the r11 disk-write assertions to cover:
  - `_build/**` (compiled artifacts and dependency state)
  - `cover/**` (coverage instrumentation output)
  - `/tmp` writes via temp file enumeration before / after
  - Host project config (mix.exs, mix.lock, .formatter.exs)
- Add explicit allowed-write list with rationale per directory

## Allowed touches
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs
- (possibly) test/support helpers for disk-snapshot diffing
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md, coverage.spec.md (r11 contract update)
- README.md, CHANGELOG.md

## Out of Scope
- Block runtime disk writes via filesystem sandbox (not feasible in BEAM)
- Add disk-write-tracking telemetry (.30 territory)

## Merge gate
- [ ] mix test green; r11 tests now cover broader surface
- [ ] Auditor APPROVE

Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Commit: 942d8a7c1bde97eeacd11fbae681f3987eee4d88
Files touched:
- test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs
- test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs
- test/support/disk_snapshot_helper.exs (new)
- .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md
- .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md
- README.md
- CHANGELOG.md

Tests added:
- MutagenEx.MutationRunnerTest "r11: no disk writes (broader surface)" /
  "lib/, _build/, cover/, host config, and /tmp are byte-identical
  before/after a runner pass"
- MutagenEx.CoverageRunnerTest "r7: no disk writes (broader surface, s7)" /
  "lib/, _build/, cover/, host config, and /tmp are byte-identical
  before/after a coverage run"
- MutagenEx.TestSupport.DiskSnapshot helper module (snapshot/1, diff/2,
  mutagen_attributable_tmp/2) — provides the disk-snapshot diffing primitive
  the two broadened tests share.

Verification stubs flipped: none (v2 covering r11 in
mutation_pipeline.spec.md and v4 covering r7 in coverage.spec.md were
already execute: true; the test files those stubs point at now carry the
broader assertions).

Summary: The r11 and r7 disk-write tests were broadened from
`lib/**/*.ex`-only SHA-256 checks to a snapshot-diff over `lib/`,
`_build/**/*.{beam,app}`, `cover/**`, host project config (mix.exs,
mix.lock, .formatter.exs), and `/tmp` entries with a `mutagen_ex_`
prefix. The shared helper at `test/support/disk_snapshot_helper.exs`
encapsulates the snapshot/diff logic with documented allowed-write
rationale per surface. The two spec statements
(mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r11, mutagen.coverage.r7) were updated to name
the broader surface explicitly with `Allowed-write list: NONE`. `mix
compile --warnings-as-errors` clean; full `mix test` is 362/0 green.

VERIFY: PASS
Session: 866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0
Log: ~/.claude-personal/projects/-Users-mz-dev-mz-mutagen-ex/866c9970-ee52-41f7-b573-aa2ec26231c0.jsonl

Mechanical checks:
- Files touched: 7
- Files outside Allowed globs: 0
- Files matching Out-of-Scope globs: 0
  - .spec/specs/coverage.spec.md            -> Allowed (.spec/specs/coverage.spec.md)
  - .spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md   -> Allowed (.spec/specs/mutation_pipeline.spec.md)
  - CHANGELOG.md                            -> Allowed (CHANGELOG.md)
  - README.md                               -> Allowed (README.md)
  - test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs  -> Allowed
  - test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs  -> Allowed
  - test/support/disk_snapshot_helper.exs   -> Allowed ("test/support helpers for disk-snapshot diffing")

Semantic checks:
- Spec stubs run: 3 (mutation_pipeline.v2, coverage.v1, mutation_pipeline.v3 == coverage.v4 [c1_test.exs]), passed: 3
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs -> 22/0 (covers r11/v2)
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs -> 12/0 (covers r7/v1)
  - mix test test/mutagen_ex/integration/c1_test.exs  -> 1/0  (covers r11/v3 and r7/v4)
- Requirements coverage (one line per touched must requirement):
  - mutagen.mutation_pipeline.r11: covered by test/mutagen_ex/mutation_runner_test.exs:1010-1058 ("r11: no disk writes (broader surface)"). Would fail if the runner mutated mix.exs / .formatter.exs / wrote a stray .beam under _build/ / dropped a cover/ report / left a /tmp entry prefixed mutagen_ex_ — the four diff.{modified, removed, added, attributable} assertions catch each surface independently with named error messages.
  - mutagen.coverage.r7: covered by test/mutagen_ex/coverage_runner_test.exs:317-355 ("r7: no disk writes (broader surface, s7)"). Would fail if CoverageRunner.run/1 or AstCache.load/1 wrote a coverage report under cover/, left cover-instrumented .beam on disk under _build/, or rewrote any host config file — same four-axis diff assertions over the same surfaces.
- Intent-level scope concerns: none. No runtime disk-write sandboxing introduced (out of scope), no disk-write telemetry added (that's .30 territory). The helper is a pure-passive observer (hash + compare) invoked from test code only.
- Project verification: PASS — mix compile --warnings-as-errors clean; mix test = 362 tests, 0 failures (13 excluded e2e_slow).
- Audit-expanded scope (per step 11), if any: none — no prior AUDIT: BOUNCE comments on this ticket.

Source-claim tracing (step 10):
- (a) Helper hashes the named directories: CONFIRMED. test/support/disk_snapshot_helper.exs:67-73 defines default_globs as ["lib/**/*.ex", "lib/**/*.exs", "_build/**/*.beam", "_build/**/*.app", "cover/**/*"]; @host_config_files :61-65 = ["mix.exs", "mix.lock", ".formatter.exs"]; hash_globs/1 :165-173 and hash_host_configs/1 :175-181 both run :crypto.hash(:sha256, File.read!(path)) on regular files. Surface matches the spec verbatim.
- (b) Tests actually USE the helper to assert empty diffs: CONFIRMED. Both r11 and r7 tests call snapshot/0 pre and post, compute diff/2, then assert diff.modified == [], diff.removed == [], diff.added == [], and mutagen_attributable_tmp(diff) == [] — four independent assertions per test, each with a named error message that enumerates the offending paths.
- (c) /tmp filter scoped tightly enough: CONFIRMED. list_tmp_entries/1 :183-188 enumerates only top-level entries (File.ls, not recursive walk) — avoids hashing /tmp content which would be flaky. mutagen_attributable_tmp/2 :159-161 filters tmp_added by String.starts_with?(&1, "mutagen_ex") (default prefix). Concurrent test processes adding unrelated /tmp entries are correctly ignored; only runner-attributable entries trip the assertion. The prefix matches the spec's "mutagen_ex_"-attributable language (the filter uses "mutagen_ex" which captures both "mutagen_ex_" and "mutagen_ex" forms — superset of the spec, not a gap).

Verdict: PASS

Notes:
- Helper loaded via Code.require_file rather than test/support/ elixirc_path — keeps mix.exs untouched (out of Allowed touches) at the cost of two load sites. Idempotent per file comments. Reasonable trade.
- The helper's docstring mentions a "diff_build/2" helper that is not actually implemented (only diff/2 exists). Dead reference in the @moduledoc — minor doc polish target, not a correctness issue. The tests themselves correctly use diff/2.
- Implementer's TestSupport module name is MutagenEx.TestSupport.DiskSnapshot but the file is disk_snapshot_helper.exs (not the conventional disk_snapshot.exs). Convention-only, harmless.
- The r11/r7 spec statements now explicitly enumerate the broader surfaces with Allowed-write list: NONE — future code that needs scratch space is forced to declare its writes against the spec, which is the intended forcing function.

title: Broaden r11 disk-write tests beyond lib/**/*.ex
